name: Bennie Green
abstract: Bennie Green (April 16, 1923 – March 23, 1977) was an American jazz trombonist. Born in Chicago, Illinois, United States, Green worked in the orchestras of Earl Hines and Charlie Ventura, and recorded as bandleader through the 1950s and 1960s.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: Now, we had Ike Quebec and Dave Young in the reeds... Then we had Bennie Green on trombone.

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Jay and Kai was originally supposed to be Jay and Bennie Green... The original game plan was for Bennie Green and J.J. Johnson to team up... This is the Capitol recording which had you and Bennie Green
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: Bennie Green was playing trombone. And it really made for a nice ensemble sound. That is one of the jobs that I wanted that I was seeking.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: The job with Charlie Ventura. Bennie Green had been there and he was about to leave, so I really wanted a small situation to play in.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Bennie was in the Army, stationed close by, and I was in the Navy of course. We used to meet. We used to hit the sauce pretty good.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Keter Betts
about_quotes.evidence: Kenny O’Brien went on to go with Charlie Ventura, he was with Charlie Ventura, and Jackie and Roy, and Bennie Green
about_quotes.doc_title: Keter Betts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996
